commit.message:
Merge PR #2109

Track whether a successful result event was seen during stream-json parsing.
When the process exits with an error but a successful result was previously
observed, override to Success=true instead of marking as failure. This prevents
false negatives when Claude Code completes work but times out on a subsequent
turn (e.g., writing a final summary).

commit.message:
Merge PR #2106

Add `heartbeat_timeout` field to executor BackendConfig, allowing users
to tune how long Pilot waits for stream-json events before killing a
hung subprocess. Default remains 5m, valid range is 1m-30m.

Changes:
- Rename HeartbeatTimeout constant to DefaultHeartbeatTimeout
- Add Min/MaxHeartbeatTimeout bounds constants
- Add HeartbeatTimeout field to BackendConfig (yaml: heartbeat_timeout)
- Add EffectiveHeartbeatTimeout() method with clamping
- Wire timeout through backend factory to ClaudeCode and QwenCode backends
- Add SetHeartbeatTimeout() to both backend types
- Table-driven tests for validation, defaults, and factory wiring

commit.message:
Merge PR #2088

* feat(github): add SearchOpenSubIssues method (GH-2085)

Add SearchOpenSubIssues(ctx, owner, repo, parentNum) to GitHub client
that uses the search API to count open issues referencing a parent via
"Parent: GH-{num}" body text. Table-driven tests cover: open siblings,
all closed, and API error cases.

* feat(autopilot): auto-close parent issue when all sub-issues done (GH-2086)

Add maybeCloseParentIssue() to controller that runs at the end of
handleMerged(). It fetches the sub-issue body, parses the parent
reference, checks for remaining open siblings via SearchOpenSubIssues,
and if none remain: adds pilot-done label, removes pilot-failed and
pilot-in-progress labels, posts a summary comment, and closes the
parent issue. All errors are logged as warnings without blocking merge.

Tests cover: last sub-issue triggers close, siblings still open,
no parent reference, no issue number, API errors, and label cleanup.

commit.message:
Merge PR #2083

Add StageReviewRequested stage and ReviewFeedback config to handle
PR review change requests. When a reviewer requests changes, autopilot
creates a revision issue with formatted review feedback, learns from
the review, closes the PR, and deletes the branch.

Types:
- StageReviewRequested stage constant
- ReviewFeedbackConfig with Enabled and MaxIterations

Feedback loop:
- FailureReviewRequested failure type
- CreateReviewIssue() creates issues with formatted review feedback
- formatReviewFeedback() groups comments by file in <details> blocks
  with 4000-char truncation

Controller:
- handleReviewRequested() orchestrates the full flow
- hasChangesRequested() filters bot reviews and old reviews by time
- OnReviewRequested() transitions tracked PRs to review_requested stage
- Wired into ProcessPR() switch and processAllPRs() polling detection

Tests: 10 new tests (6 controller, 4 feedback loop)

commit.message:
Merge PR #2071

* feat(memory): add eval regression detection with pass@1 comparison (GH-2064)

Add CheckRegression() that compares baseline and current eval task lists,
computes pass@1 rates, identifies regressed/improved task IDs, and flags
regressions exceeding a configurable threshold (default 5pp).

Table-driven tests cover: no regression, minor/major drops, improvement,
empty inputs, and identical inputs.

* fix(memory): remove unused currentSuccess map in eval regression

* feat(alerts): add eval regression alert type and handler (GH-2065)

Add AlertTypeEvalRegression and EventTypeEvalRegression for detecting
eval pass@1 score regressions. The handler reads baseline_pass1,
current_pass1, delta, regressed_count, and recommendation from event
metadata. Severity escalates to critical when delta exceeds 2× the
configured threshold. Includes default rule and parseAlertType mapping.

* feat(dashboard): add eval stats panel with pass@1 rate and regression warning (GH-2066)

Add renderEvalStats() method that displays a compact EVAL panel showing:
- Current pass@1 rate computed from all eval tasks
- Trend indicator (↑/↓/→) comparing recent vs older task halves
- Regression warning in red when pass rate drops exceed threshold

Wired into renderDashboard() between autopilot and history panels.
Includes table-driven tests for nil store, empty tasks, rate display,
and regression warning rendering.

* fix(dashboard): restore renderHistory doc comment displaced by eval panel insertion

* feat(cli): add eval command group with regression check subcommand (GH-2067)

Add `pilot eval check` CLI command that compares pass@1 rates between
two eval runs and exits with code 1 on regression (CI-friendly).

- Create eval.go with newEvalCmd() command group and eval check subcommand
- Flags: --baseline <run-id>, --current <run-id>, --threshold <float>
- Loads eval tasks from memory.Store by execution_id
- Calls memory.CheckRegression() and prints formatted report
- Emits EventTypeEvalRegression alert when regression detected
- Add ExecutionID filter to EvalTaskFilter for run-based queries
- Register eval command in main.go
- Add tests for command structure, flag validation, and report output

commit.message:
Merge PR #2024

* feat(memory): add pattern_performance table with contextual confidence (GH-2020)

Add pattern_performance SQLite table tracking per-pattern success/failure
rates by project and task type. Implement RecordPatternOutcome() with UPSERT
on the unique constraint and GetContextualConfidence() combining project-specific
success rate with recency decay formula (1/(1+daysSince/30)). Includes
table-driven tests for fresh defaults, decay at 90+ days, cross-project
divergence, mixed task types, and UPSERT accumulation.

* feat(memory): use contextual confidence for pattern ranking and record outcomes (GH-2021)

Replace global pattern.Confidence with GetContextualConfidence() when ranking
patterns for prompt injection. Patterns are now sorted by project+task-type
success rate with recency decay, falling back to global confidence when no
contextual data exists.

Add RecordPatternOutcome() calls in runner.go after each task execution
completes, recording success/failure for all project-linked patterns with
the inferred task type and model name.

commit.message:
Merge PR #2019

* feat(memory): add execution learning with relations and keyword search (GH-2014)

AddExecutionLearning creates learning nodes with relations linking
task→files, files→patterns, patterns→outcome. GetRelatedByKeywords
searches across title/content/metadata with case-insensitive matching,
returning results sorted by recency.

* feat(executor): integrate knowledge graph into execution pipeline (GH-2015)

Add KnowledgeGraphRecorder interface and wire it into Runner for
bidirectional knowledge graph integration:

- Post-execution: recordGraphLearning() calls AddExecutionLearning()
  with task title, description, extracted patterns, and outcome
- Pre-execution: BuildPrompt() queries GetRelatedByKeywords() and
  injects up to 5 related learnings into the prompt
- Add SetKnowledgeGraph()/HasKnowledgeGraph() setters following
  existing SetLearningLoop pattern
- 13 unit tests covering runner integration, prompt injection,
  nil safety, error handling, cap enforcement, and interface compliance

* style(executor): fix indentation in prompt_builder knowledge graph block (GH-2015)

* feat(executor): wire knowledge graph into runner in both polling and gateway modes (GH-2016)

Initialize KnowledgeGraph from cfg.Memory.Path and call
runner.SetKnowledgeGraph() alongside existing SetLearningLoop() and
SetPatternContext() calls. Wired in both polling mode and gateway mode
to avoid the parity gap noted in GH-1935. Also updated test harness.

commit.message:
Merge PR #1987

* feat(poller): add merged PR guard to prevent retry of completed work (GH-1983)

When pilot-failed is removed from an issue, the poller previously
re-dispatched even if merged PRs already existed, causing duplicate
sub-issues and empty-push failures. This adds a hasMergedWork() guard
that uses GitHub Search API to detect merged PRs with GH-{number} in
title before retrying, in both parallel and sequential poller paths.

* fix(poller): use tagged switch in merged-PR-guard tests (GH-1986)

staticcheck QF1002: convert `switch { case x == y: }` to `switch x { case y: }`
in TestPoller_CheckForNewIssues_SkipsRetryWithMergedPRs and
TestPoller_FindOldestUnprocessedIssue_SkipsRetryWithMergedPRs.

commit.message:
Merge PR #1980

Replace generic extractCIErrorPatterns delegation with 15 dedicated CI-specific
matchers organized into 4 categories: compilation (undefined identifiers, type
mismatches, unused vars, missing returns), test (FAIL lines, timeouts, assertion
failures, runtime panics), lint (golangci-lint, staticcheck, errcheck), and
build (missing modules, version conflicts, import cycles, missing deps).

Each pattern is tagged with source:ci, category:<type>, and optional check names.
LearnFromCIFailure now passes check names directly to the extractor via variadic
parameter. Empty/whitespace logs return nil gracefully.

Table-driven tests cover all 4 categories, check name tagging, empty logs,
whitespace-only logs, and mixed-category scenarios.

commit.message:
Merge PR #1975

In SaveExtractedPatterns, detect recurring anti-patterns by title match.
When a CI-sourced pattern (source:ci context) recurs, boost confidence
by 1.5x (capped at 0.95) instead of creating a duplicate. CI-sourced
anti-patterns are also persisted as CrossPatterns in SQLite so they
appear in PatternContext.InjectPatterns() output.

Adds three tests: unit test for boost logic, cap verification, and an
end-to-end integration test covering CI failure → extract → recurrence →
confidence boost → FormatForPrompt visibility.

Fix: Add OnlyAnti field to PatternQuery so the anti-pattern query only
returns anti-patterns without competing against normal patterns for the
MaxResults cap. Also add missing IncludeGlobal=true to match
GetRelevantPatterns behavior.

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): raise auto-size thresholds for cleaner git graph

Full mode (SHA + author) only at graphWidth >= 65 (was 46).
Medium (refs + message) at >= 40 (was 28).
Small (message only) below 40.

Keeps the graph compact at typical terminal widths.

fix(dashboard): auto-size git graph based on terminal width

Graph now auto-selects small/medium/full based on available space:
- < 28 chars: small (graph + message only)
- 28-45 chars: medium (graph + refs + message)
- > 45 chars: full (graph + refs + message + SHA + author)

'g' key toggles graph on/off; size adapts automatically on resize.

feat(dashboard): restore git graph size variants — small, medium, large

v2.30 had small/large modes but small was dropped during stacked layout
refactor. Restore 3 display sizes with proper responsiveness:

- Small: graph + message only, capped at 32 chars, title "GIT"
- Medium: graph + refs + message (no SHA/author), capped at 50 chars
- Large: full details with SHA + author, title "GIT GRAPH"

Toggle cycle: Hidden → Full → Medium → Small → Hidden
Mode-aware stacked breakpoints for narrow terminals.

fix(dashboard): sync isStackedMode breakpoint with View layout (GH-1909)

isStackedMode() used old threshold (90 cols) while View() used new
threshold (120 cols). Dashboard panels stayed at 69 chars even when
graph was stacked below at full width. Now both use the same breakpoint.

Merge PR #1913

* fix(dashboard): stretch dashboard panels to full width in stacked/narrow mode

When in stacked mode (git graph visible + narrow terminal), dashboard panels
(QUEUE, AUTOPILOT, HISTORY, LOGS, metrics cards) now stretch to full terminal
width instead of staying at fixed 69 chars. This matches the git graph panel
width for visual consistency.

Approach: Added effectivePanelTotalWidth() and related methods to Model that
return the terminal width in stacked mode, or the default panelTotalWidth
otherwise. All panel builder and card builder functions now accept explicit
width parameters instead of using hardcoded constants.

Closes #1909

* fix(dashboard): remove unused effective width helper methods

Remove effectivePanelInnerWidth, effectiveCardWidth, and
effectiveCardInnerWidth methods that were never called.
The implementation passes width parameters directly to panel/card
builders instead. Fixes golangci-lint unused errors from CI.

Closes #1912

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): stack git graph below dashboard on narrow terminals

When terminal width < 90 cols (too narrow for side-by-side), the git
graph panel now renders below the dashboard at dashboard width instead
of being silently hidden. Works in Zellij splits and small terminals.

fix(dashboard): revert narrow-terminal guard, keep only footer fix

v2.38.2 wrongly hid "g: graph" hint and blocked the key on narrow
terminals. Reverted — "g" always toggles, hint always shows.
The real fix is the height-truncation change: help footer now renders
after line capping so it's never cut off in short panes.

fix(dashboard): git graph silently broken on narrow/short terminals

Two bugs fixed:
1. Pressing "g" on terminals narrower than 90 cols changed internal
   state but rendered nothing — now blocks the toggle and hides the
   hint from the help footer.
2. Help footer was always the first line cut by height truncation,
   making keyboard hints invisible in small panes (e.g. Zellij).
   Footer is now rendered after truncation so it's always visible.
docs: add desktop app installation to README

fix: remove SQLite WAL artifacts breaking Windows builds

The :memory: directory contains SQLite shared-memory and WAL files
that were accidentally committed. Colons in paths are invalid on
Windows, causing checkout failures in CI (GHA build-desktop job).

Added *.db-shm and *.db-wal to .gitignore alongside existing *.db rule.

releases.name: pilot v2.79.2
releases.body:
## fix(executor): skip Navigator auto-init in LocalMode

LocalMode tasks (`--local`) no longer trigger `maybeInitNavigator()`. Previously, running a local task would create an `.agent/` directory unnecessarily, wasting setup time and potentially hijacking the prompt to the Navigator path instead of the LocalMode problem-solving prompt.

**PR:** #2110 | **Issue:** GH-2108

releases.name: pilot v2.79.1
releases.body:
## fix(executor): recover success when Claude Code times out after completing work

When Claude Code completes work (tests pass, code committed) but times out on a subsequent turn (e.g., writing a final summary), the process exits with code 1. Pilot previously marked this as a failure, creating false negatives and triggering unnecessary retries.

Now tracks whether a successful `result` event was seen during stream-json parsing. If the process exits with an error but work was already completed, the result is overridden to success.

**PR:** #2109 | **Issue:** GH-2107

releases.name: pilot v2.79.0
releases.body:
## feat(executor): configurable HeartbeatTimeout via config.yaml

Heartbeat timeout is now configurable via `executor.heartbeat_timeout` in config.yaml instead of being hardcoded. Allows tuning for environments where Claude Code takes longer between output events (e.g., complex reasoning tasks).

Also fixes heartbeat timer not being cancelled when a successful result event arrives — previously could kill the process after it already finished.

**PR:** #2106 | **Issue:** GH-2104

releases.name: pilot v2.78.1
releases.body:
## fix(executor): LocalMode prompt priority + heartbeat cancel on result

`task.LocalMode` is now checked **before** Navigator detection in `BuildPrompt()`. Previously, sandbox environments (bench, CI) with `.agent/` directories would hijack the prompt to the Navigator path, breaking LocalMode's problem-solving prompt.

Also cancels heartbeat timer when a result event is received, preventing false timeout kills after task completion.

**PR:** #2105 | **Issue:** GH-2103

releases.name: pilot v2.78.0
releases.body:
## feat: Add `pilot init` command for project scaffolding

New `pilot init` command scaffolds a project with config file, Navigator `.agent/` structure, and git hooks in one step. Simplifies onboarding for new projects.

**PR:** #2102 | **Issue:** GH-2100

readme_html:
The Problem

You have 47 tickets in your backlog. You agonize over which to prioritize. Half are "quick fixes" that somehow take 2 hours each. Your PM asks for status updates. Sound familiar?

The Solution

Pilot picks up tickets from GitHub, Linear, Jira, or Asana—plans the implementation, writes the code, runs tests, and opens a PR. You review and merge. That's it.

┌─────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐
│   Ticket    │ ───▶ │   Pilot     │ ───▶ │   Review    │ ───▶ │   Ship      │
│  (GitHub)   │      │  (AI dev)   │      │   (You)     │      │  (Merge)    │
└─────────────┘      └─────────────┘      └─────────────┘      └─────────────┘

Install

Homebrew (recommended)

brew tap alekspetrov/pilot
brew install pilot

Go Install

go install github.com/alekspetrov/pilot/cmd/pilot@latest

From Source

git clone https://github.com/alekspetrov/pilot
cd pilot
make build
sudo make install-global

Desktop App

Download the desktop app from the latest release:

Platform Download
macOS (Universal) Pilot-Desktop-macOS-universal.dmg
Windows (x64) Pilot-Desktop-Windows-amd64-setup.exe
Linux (x64) Pilot-Desktop-Linux-amd64.tar.gz

Requirements

  • Go 1.22+ (build only)
  • Claude Code CLI 2.1.17+
  • OpenAI API key (optional, for voice transcription)

Quick Start

# 1. Initialize config
pilot init

# 2. Start Pilot
pilot start --github              # GitHub issue polling
pilot start --telegram            # Telegram bot
pilot start --telegram --github   # Both

# 3. Create a GitHub issue with 'pilot' label, or message your Telegram bot

That's it. Go grab coffee. ☕

How It Works

You label issue "pilot"
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Pilot claims it  │  ← Adds "pilot/in-progress" label
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Creates branch   │  ← pilot/GH-{number}
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Plans approach   │  ← Analyzes codebase, designs solution
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Implements       │  ← Writes code with Claude Code
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Quality gates    │  ← Test, lint, build validation
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌───────────────────┐
│  Opens PR         │  ← Links to issue, adds "pilot/done"
└───────┬───────────┘
        │
        ▼
    You review
        │
        ▼
      Merge 🚀

Features

133 features implemented across execution, intelligence, integrations, and infrastructure.

Core Execution

Feature Description
Autopilot CI monitoring, auto-merge, feedback loop (dev/stage/prod modes)
Epic Decomposition Complex tasks auto-split into sequential subtasks via Haiku API
Self-Review Auto code review before PR push catches issues early
Sequential Execution Wait for PR merge before next issue (prevents conflicts)
Quality Gates Test/lint/build validation with auto-retry
Execution Replay Record, playback, analyze, export (HTML/JSON/MD)

Intelligence

Feature Description
Model Routing Haiku (trivial) → Opus 4.6 (standard/complex), auto-detected
Effort Routing Maps task complexity to Claude thinking depth
Research Subagents Haiku-powered parallel codebase exploration
Navigator Integration Auto-detected .agent/, skipped for trivial tasks
Cross-Project Memory Shared patterns and context across repositories

Integrations

Feature Description
Telegram Bot Chat, research, planning, tasks + voice & images
GitHub Polling Auto-pick issues with pilot label
GitLab / Azure DevOps Full polling + webhook adapters
Linear/Jira/Asana Webhooks and task sync
Daily Briefs Scheduled reports via Slack/Email/Telegram
Alerting Task failures, cost thresholds, stuck detection

Infrastructure

Feature Description
Dashboard TUI Sparkline metrics cards, queue depth, autopilot status
Persistent Metrics Token/cost/task counts survive restarts via SQLite
Hot Upgrade Self-update with pilot upgrade or u key in dashboard
Cost Controls Budget limits with hard enforcement
Multiple Backends Claude Code + OpenCode support
BYOK Bring your own Anthropic key, Bedrock, or Vertex

Autopilot Modes

Control how much autonomy Pilot has:

# Fast iteration - skip CI, auto-merge
pilot start --autopilot=dev --github

# Balanced - wait for CI, then auto-merge
pilot start --autopilot=stage --github

# Safe - wait for CI + human approval
pilot start --autopilot=prod --github

Telegram Integration

Talk to Pilot naturally - it understands different interaction modes:

Mode Example What Happens
💬 Chat "What do you think about using Redis?" Conversational response, no code changes
🔍 Question "What files handle authentication?" Quick read-only answer
🔬 Research "Research how the caching layer works" Deep analysis sent to chat
📐 Planning "Plan how to add rate limiting" Shows plan with Execute/Cancel buttons
🚀 Task "Add rate limiting to /api/users" Confirms, then creates PR 
You: "Plan how to add user authentication"
Pilot: 📐 Drafting plan...
Pilot: 📋 Implementation Plan
       1. Create auth middleware...
       2. Add JWT token validation...
       [Execute] [Cancel]

You: [clicks Execute]
Pilot: 🚀 Executing...
Pilot: ✅ PR #142 ready: https://github.com/...

Send voice messages, images, or text. Pilot understands context.

Dashboard

Real-time visibility into what Pilot is doing:

┌─ Pilot Dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────────┐

│  Status: ● Running    Autopilot: stage    Queue: 3        │

pilot start --dashboard --github

Environment Variables

Pilot uses Claude Code for AI execution:

Variable Description
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Custom Anthropic API key (uses your own account)
ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL Custom API endpoint (proxies, enterprise)
CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK Set to 1 for AWS Bedrock
CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX Set to 1 for Google Vertex AI

Example: Using AWS Bedrock

export CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1
export AWS_REGION=us-east-1
pilot start --github

Configuration

Config location: ~/.pilot/config.yaml

version: "1.0"

gateway:
  host: "127.0.0.1"
  port: 9090

adapters:
  telegram:
    enabled: true
    bot_token: "${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}"
    chat_id: "${TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID}"

  github:
    enabled: true
    token: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    repo: "owner/repo"
    pilot_label: "pilot"
    polling:
      enabled: true
      interval: 30s

orchestrator:
  execution:
    mode: sequential           # "sequential" or "parallel"
    wait_for_merge: true       # Wait for PR merge before next task
    poll_interval: 30s
    pr_timeout: 1h

projects:
  - name: "my-project"
    path: "~/Projects/my-project"
    navigator: true
    default_branch: main

daily_brief:
  enabled: true
  schedule: "0 8 * * *"
  timezone: "Europe/Berlin"

alerts:
  enabled: true
  channels:
    - name: telegram-alerts
      type: telegram
      severities: [critical, error, warning]

executor:
  backend: claude-code          # "claude-code" or "opencode"

CLI Reference

Core Commands

pilot start          # Start with configured inputs
pilot stop           # Stop daemon
pilot status         # Show running tasks
pilot init           # Initialize configuration
pilot version        # Show version info

pilot start

pilot start                          # Config-driven
pilot start --telegram               # Enable Telegram polling
pilot start --github                 # Enable GitHub issue polling
pilot start --linear                 # Enable Linear webhooks
pilot start --telegram --github      # Enable both
pilot start --dashboard              # With TUI dashboard
pilot start --no-gateway             # Polling only (no HTTP server)
pilot start --sequential             # Sequential execution mode
pilot start --autopilot=stage        # Autopilot mode (dev/stage/prod)
pilot start -p ~/Projects/myapp      # Specify project
pilot start --replace                # Kill existing instance first

pilot task

pilot task "Add user authentication"                    # Run in cwd
pilot task "Fix login bug" -p ~/Projects/myapp          # Specify project
pilot task "Refactor API" --verbose                     # Stream output
pilot task "Update docs" --dry-run                      # Preview only
pilot task "Implement feature" --backend opencode       # Use OpenCode

pilot upgrade

pilot upgrade                    # Check and upgrade
pilot upgrade check              # Only check for updates
pilot upgrade rollback           # Restore previous version
pilot upgrade --force            # Skip task completion wait
pilot upgrade --no-restart       # Don't restart after upgrade
pilot upgrade --yes              # Skip confirmation

Analytics Commands

pilot brief                       # Show scheduler status
pilot brief --now                 # Generate and send immediately
pilot brief --weekly              # Generate weekly summary

pilot metrics summary             # Last 7 days overview
pilot metrics summary --days 30   # Last 30 days
pilot metrics daily               # Daily breakdown
pilot metrics projects            # Per-project stats

pilot usage summary               # Billable usage summary
pilot usage daily                 # Daily breakdown
pilot usage export --format json  # Export for billing

pilot patterns list               # List learned patterns
pilot patterns search "auth"      # Search by keyword

Development

make deps        # Install dependencies
make build       # Build binary
make test        # Run tests
make lint        # Run linter
make dev         # Development mode with hot reload

FAQ

Pilot runs in your environment with your permissions. It can only access repos you configure. All changes go through PR review (unless you enable auto-merge). You stay in control.

Pilot is free. You pay for Claude API usage (~$0.50-2.00 per typical task). Set budget limits to control costs.

Best for: bug fixes, small features, refactoring, tests, docs, dependency updates.

Not ideal for: large architectural changes, security-critical code, tasks requiring human judgment.

Yes. Pilot uses Navigator to understand your patterns, conventions, and architecture. Cross-project memory shares learnings across repositories.
